title: MLIR Circuit IR
summary: A chain of 3 modules implementing hardware compilation infrastructure. MLIR provides the core IR with parser, types, operations, regions, and passes. CIRCT adds hardware-specific dialects (HW, Comb, Seq, FSM) with simulation. K-CIRCT extends with kernel-level formal semantics, HLS scheduling, and dataflow analysis for hardware acceleration.
instructionPreview: Implement a three-layer system in Python covering compiler infrastructure (MLIR), hardware description (CIRCT), and formal verification (K-CIRCT):
instruction:
Implement a three-layer system in Python covering compiler infrastructure (MLIR),
hardware description (CIRCT), and formal verification (K-CIRCT):

1. MLIR module (src/mlir/): Implement core IR types (Operation, Block, Region,
   Module), a text parser for MLIR syntax, a printer, a pass manager, and a greedy
   pattern rewrite driver with constant-folding patterns for arith dialect ops.
   Parse inputs/sample_arith.mlir and report function count, total ops, fold count,
   and printed output line count.

2. CIRCT module (src/circt/): Implement hw/comb/seq hardware dialects with a parser
   for CIRCT-level MLIR text and a cycle-accurate circuit simulator. Parse
   inputs/alu.mlir and inputs/counter.mlir; simulate ALU operations and counter cycles.

3. K-CIRCT module (src/k_circt/): Implement K-framework inspired formal semantics
   with static analysis (symbol/type resolution), effectful circuit state tracking,
   and a Kimulator that evaluates circuits using semantic rules while recording traces.

Working directory: /workspace
Run tests: python3 tests/test_outputs.py (from the task root)
Each module contributes KEY: VALUE result lines via src/run_experiments.py.
